title: Upsert
summary: Insert a row or update existing row on key conflict.

tsql: MERGE INTO users AS target
USING (SELECT @id AS id, @name AS name) AS source
ON target.id = source.id
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET name = source.name, updated_at = SYSUTCDATETIME()
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (id, name, updated_at) VALUES (source.id, source.name, SYSUTCDATETIME());

common: Use unique keys to define conflict target and avoid duplicates.

notes: MERGE can have race-condition caveats in some SQL Server workloads.

notes.tsql: Use MERGE or separate UPDATE/INSERT transaction-safe pattern.

title: Pagination
summary: Page results with LIMIT/OFFSET or FETCH syntax.

tsql: SELECT ...
FROM ...
ORDER BY stable_key
OFFSET @offset ROWS FETCH NEXT @page_size ROWS ONLY

common: SELECT id, name FROM users ORDER BY id LIMIT 25 OFFSET 50;
tsql: SELECT id, name FROM users ORDER BY id OFFSET 50 ROWS FETCH NEXT 25 ROWS ONLY;

notes: Prefer keyset pagination for deep scrolling.

title: Top N per Group
summary: Pick the top rows within each partition/group.

common: SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY group_key ORDER BY metric DESC) AS rn
SELECT * FROM ranked WHERE rn <= N
WITH ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) rn FROM employees) SELECT * FROM ranked WHERE rn <= 3;

notes: Requires window functions; add index on (group_key, metric DESC).

title: Latest Row per Key
summary: Get most recent record for each entity key.

common: SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY entity_id ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC) AS rn
WITH ranked AS (SELECT *, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC) rn FROM login_events) SELECT * FROM ranked WHERE rn = 1;

title: Conditional Aggregation
summary: Compute multiple filtered metrics in one grouped query.

common: SUM(CASE WHEN condition1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS metric1,
SUM(CASE WHEN condition2 THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS metric2
SELECT customer_id, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'paid' THEN total ELSE 0 END) AS paid_total, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'refunded' THEN total ELSE 0 END) AS refunded_total FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id;

notes: Single scan can outperform multiple joined aggregate subqueries.

title: Delete with Join
summary: Delete rows based on matches in another table.

common: DELETE FROM target WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM other WHERE join_condition)

mysql: DELETE t FROM target t JOIN other o ON join_condition
tsql: DELETE t FROM target t JOIN other o ON join_condition

postgres: DELETE FROM sessions s WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users u WHERE u.id = s.user_id AND u.deleted_at IS NOT NULL);
mysql: DELETE s FROM sessions s JOIN users u ON u.id = s.user_id WHERE u.deleted_at IS NOT NULL;
sqlite: DELETE FROM sessions WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM users WHERE users.id = sessions.user_id AND users.deleted_at IS NOT NULL);
tsql: DELETE s FROM sessions s JOIN users u ON u.id = s.user_id WHERE u.deleted_at IS NOT NULL;

notes: Validate the matched set with SELECT before DELETE.

title: Recursive CTE
summary: Traverse hierarchical data like org charts and category trees.

common: SELECT id, parent_id, name, 0 AS depth FROM nodes WHERE parent_id IS NULL
SELECT n.id, n.parent_id, n.name, t.depth + 1 FROM nodes n JOIN tree t ON n.parent_id = t.id

WITH RECURSIVE tree AS (SELECT id, parent_id, name, 0 depth FROM categories WHERE parent_id IS NULL UNION ALL SELECT c.id, c.parent_id, c.name, t.depth + 1 FROM categories c JOIN tree t ON c.parent_id = t.id) SELECT * FROM tree ORDER BY depth, id;

notes: Protect against cycles and set recursion limits where supported.

title: Pivot Emulation
summary: Turn row categories into columns using conditional aggregation.

common: SUM(CASE WHEN category = 'A' THEN value ELSE 0 END) AS a_value,
SUM(CASE WHEN category = 'B' THEN value ELSE 0 END) AS b_value

tsql: SELECT entity_id, [A], [B]
FROM (SELECT entity_id, category, value FROM t) src
PIVOT (SUM(value) FOR category IN ([A], [B])) p

common: SELECT user_id, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'open' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS open_cnt, SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'closed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS closed_cnt FROM tickets GROUP BY user_id;
tsql: SELECT user_id, [open], [closed] FROM (SELECT user_id, status, 1 AS c FROM tickets) s PIVOT (SUM(c) FOR status IN ([open], [closed])) p;

notes: Conditional aggregation is portable and often easier to maintain.

title: Keyset Pagination
summary: Use last-seen sort key instead of OFFSET for scalable pagination.

common: ORDER BY sort_key DESC, id DESC

tsql: SELECT TOP (@page_size) ...
FROM ...
WHERE (sort_key < @last_sort_key) OR (sort_key = @last_sort_key AND id < @last_id)
ORDER BY sort_key DESC, id DESC

common: SELECT id, created_at, title FROM posts WHERE (created_at, id) < ('2026-01-10 12:00:00', 4002) ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC LIMIT 25;
tsql: SELECT TOP (@page_size) id, created_at, title FROM posts WHERE (created_at < @last_created_at) OR (created_at = @last_created_at AND id < @last_id) ORDER BY created_at DESC, id DESC;

notes: Requires stable ordered index matching WHERE + ORDER BY columns.
Best for infinite scroll and high page depth.

title: Top Clause (T-SQL)
summary: Use TOP as the T-SQL row-limiting primitive.

tsql: SELECT TOP (n) ... ORDER BY ...
SELECT TOP (10) id, score FROM leaderboard ORDER BY score DESC;
common: SELECT id, score FROM leaderboard ORDER BY score DESC LIMIT 10;

notes: T-SQL supports TOP WITH TIES for tie-preserving limits.
